name: Zerodha Trading Platform — Event-Driven Architecture Design
scenario: Design and document the complete Kafka event-driven architecture for the Zerodha trading platform. This is an architecture and design project (no Spring Kafka code yet — that's Topics 16.2 and 16.3). Prove the architecture by running Kafka with Docker and producing/consuming test messages via CLI.
description: Full topology design: 4 topics with schemas, partition strategy, retention, consumer groups. Hybrid architecture (sync REST + async Kafka). Throughput calculations. Verified by running Kafka locally and producing test events via kafka-console-producer.

core.description: Design 4 Kafka topics for Zerodha: trade-events, market-ticks, notification-events, audit-log. Each includes: topic name, partition count with justification, retention period with justification, partition key, and which events it carries.

Full Java event records: TradeEvent (eventId, tradeId, userId, symbol, qty, price, type, status, traceId, timestamp), MarketTickEvent, NotificationEvent, AuditEvent. All records compile with no errors.
Producer-to-consumer mapping documented as a Java class with comments: which service produces each topic, which consumer groups subscribe, and what each consumer does with the event.

Hybrid architecture diagram (text): which calls remain synchronous REST (price lookup, account verify, risk check) and which become Kafka events (notification, analytics, portfolio, audit). Justify each decision.

Run Kafka locally with Docker Compose. Create all 4 topics with correct partition counts. Produce at least 2 test trade events to trade-events. Consume from trade-events with --from-beginning and --group test-group. Include terminal output in README.

bonus.description: Throughput calculation class: given 100K trades/hour and 5ms processing time, calculate minimum consumers and partitions needed. Show with readable output.
Add a Kafka UI (Kafka UI by Provectus) to docker-compose.yml. Show the trade-events topic in the browser with messages visible. Screenshot in README.
Write a JUnit test that instantiates all 4 event records with valid data, serializes them to JSON (Jackson ObjectMapper), and deserializes back — verifying the schema round-trips correctly.

criteria: Each topic has all 5 attributes (5 pts each)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. KRaft mode: KAFKA_PROCESS_ROLES=broker,controller — no Zookeeper needed. CLUSTER_ID: run 'kafka-storage random-uuid' or use a fixed value in docker-compose. Topic creation: kafka-topics --create --partitions N --replication-factor 1. Consumer --from-beginning: reads all retained messages from offset 0. eventId field: UUID for idempotency — consumers deduplicate on this.
submission_instructions: GitHub repo with all files. README includes: topic design table, architecture diagram, terminal output of docker compose up + topic creation + produce + consume. All 4 records compile (run mvn compile).
tips_for_success: Start Kafka first: docker compose up -d && docker logs kafka (wait for 'Kafka Server started')
CLUSTER_ID: must be exactly 22 base64 characters — use 'MkU3OEVBNTcwNTJENDM2Qk' as a fixed value for dev
kafka-console-producer: type JSON, press Enter for each message, Ctrl+C to exit
kafka-console-consumer --from-beginning: reads all messages even if sent before consumer started
eventId with UUID: add to every record — essential for idempotency in Topic 16.5
concepts_reinforced: Topic design: partitions, retention, key strategy
Consumer groups for parallel and independent consumption
Hybrid architecture: sync for critical path, async for side effects
Kafka CLI: create topics, produce, consume
Message ordering within partitions via consistent key
